title: Orobus ochrus
taxonomy: Orobus ochrus is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly known as the red clover or kidney vetch. The classification of Orobus ochrus is typically as follows: genus Orobus, subgenus Orobus, tribe Loteae, subtribe Loteinae, subfamily Faboideae, and family Fabaceae. This plant is part of the Fabaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Orobus ochrus shares characteristics with other legume plants.
uses: Orobus ochrus has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, although its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven. Some sources suggest that it has been used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. In some cultures, Orobus ochrus is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, although this claim also requires further investigation. The plant's potential medicinal uses should be approached with caution until more conclusive evidence is available.
benefits: Orobus ochrus, also known as the red clover, has been traditionally used for its health benefits. The plant is rich in isoflavones, which may help reduce the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer. The antioxidants present in Orobus ochrus may also help alleviate symptoms of menopause and osteoporosis. Additionally, the plant has been used to support digestive health and may help reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm the extent of its health benefits.
properties: Orobus ochrus, also known as the yellow clover, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant's antioxidant properties may also help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, Orobus ochrus has been used to treat digestive issues and as a remedy for respiratory problems. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and its effects on human health.
constituents: Orobus ochrus is known to contain various active constituents including alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins. These compounds may contribute to its potential medicinal properties. The plant's alkaloids have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of Orobus ochrus's active constituents on human health. The plant's saponins may also play a role in its traditional use for treating various ailments.
parts: Orobus ochrus, commonly known as the bitter vetch or broom vetch, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. The plant's roots are reported to have medicinal properties and are used in herbal remedies. In traditional medicine, Orobus ochrus roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. preparations:

intro: Orobus ochrus, also known as the mountain clover or bitter vetch, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in treating various ailments including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Orobus ochrus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Orobus ochrus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
